title: A Comprehensive Survey of Autonomous LLM Agents
abstract: We present a detailed survey and taxonomy of autonomous large language model agents. We review recent developments in planning, tool use, memory architectures, and multi-agent collaboration across diverse domains.
user_goal: Understand the current landscape of LLM agents

title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Autonomous Agents on Complex Tasks
abstract: We introduce AgentBench, a multi-dimensional benchmark and leaderboard for evaluating large language models as autonomous agents. We systematically measure performance across tools, operating systems, web interaction, and reasoning environments.
user_goal: Evaluate agent capabilities across standardized benchmarks

title: Hierarchical Attention Transformers for Long Context Processing
abstract: We propose a novel hierarchical attention architecture for transformer models that efficiently processes sequence lengths up to 128k tokens. We introduce a linear complexity attention mechanism and demonstrate state-of-the-art results on long-context retrieval.

title: vServing: High-Throughput Distributed Serving for Large Language Models
abstract: We present vServing, a distributed system architecture for high-throughput LLM serving and inference. We optimize memory allocation, latency, and tensor parallelism across GPU clusters under heavy concurrent throughput.
user_goal: Optimize deployment and serving latency for LLM infrastructure

title: Theoretical Convergence Bounds for Non-Convex Gradient Optimization in Attention Layers
abstract: In this paper, we establish formal mathematical theorems and proof bounds for non-convex gradient convergence in self-attention layers. We derive exact asymptotic error rates and gradient stability conditions.
user_goal: Explore theoretical foundations of deep learning optimization
